task_completion.description: Professional bookmarks are organized into the specified folders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Chrome Bookmark Manager or Bookmarks Bar displays three folders exactly named 'CAT & Platforms', 'Medical Terminology', and 'Dictionaries & Reference'.

task_completion.description: All personal bookmarks have been removed from Chrome.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Chrome Bookmark Manager shows no personal bookmarks remaining; only the professional folders and their contents should be visible.

task_completion.description: Custom search engines with keywords 'proz' and 'ling' are added.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Chrome Settings under 'Manage search engines and site search' shows custom entries that use the keywords 'proz' and 'ling'.

task_completion.description: Spanish language support is added and bilingual spellcheck is enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Chrome Settings under 'Languages' shows Spanish in the preferred languages list, and the Spell check section shows toggles enabled for both English and Spanish.

task_completion.description: The 'Offer to translate pages' feature is globally disabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Chrome Settings under 'Languages' shows the toggle for 'Offer to translate pages that aren't in a language you read' in the OFF position.

task_completion.description: Project assets (.tmx and .pdf) are downloaded to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager window or terminal output shows the .tmx and .pdf files successfully saved inside the '~/Documents/Project_Alpha/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Chrome graphical user interface to modify settings and bookmarks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating Chrome's Settings and Bookmark Manager pages, rather than editing Chrome's JSON preference files via a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent downloaded the project assets from the local server tab in the browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the local server web page and the browser's download/save dialog, rather than using terminal commands like 'touch' or 'curl' to mock the files.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected outcomes into a text editor to trick the VLM.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual evidence of completion comes from the actual Chrome browser and system file manager interfaces, not a text document mimicking the results.
